bodyHtml:
What's available around the US

Demand centres on luxury yachts, sportfishers and sailing yachts. Typical inventory: Azimut, Sunseeker, Princess motor yachts.

  • Half-day (4h), full-day (8h), and sunset cruise (2h) rentals
  • Captain-included and self-drive options
  • Group sizes 4–14 typical; party platforms up to 18

Day rate ranges (2026)

BoatHalf-dayFull day
Pontoon 22–26 ft$350–$650$550–$1,000
Tritoon party (10–14)$500–$850$800–$1,300
Ski / wakeboard$450–$750$750–$1,200
Sport yacht 30–40 ft$1,200–$2,200$2,000–$3,800

Captain $180–$280/day. Fuel pass-through. Cleaning $40–$80.

Best time of year

Peak Memorial Day to Labor Day. Shoulder weekends 20–30% cheaper. Holiday weekends fill 4–8 weeks ahead.

Own a boat on the US? List it

List free on BoatHire24, flat 15% commission. See vs Boatsetter and vs GetMyBoat.

Nearby

faqs.q: How much does it cost to rent a boat at the US?
faqs.a: Half-day $350–$2,200 depending on boat type; full-day 50–80% more. Captain extra. Most groups spend $600–$1,400 for a half-day.

faqs.q: Do I need a boating licence?
faqs.a: Most states require a Boating Safety Course Certificate for self-drive operators born after a state cutoff. Skipper-included bypasses it.

faqs.q: BYO food and drinks?
faqs.a: Yes — almost always. Glass restrictions on some lakes.

faqs.q: Weather backup?
faqs.a: Operator-cancelled for weather = full refund or free reschedule.

faqs.q: Pontoon capacity?
faqs.a: 22–24ft: 8–10 guests; 26–28ft: 12–14; party platforms 16+. Coast Guard placard at the helm.

faqs.q: How early to book?
faqs.a: Peak summer weekends: 4–8 weeks. Holidays: 8–12 weeks. Shoulder: a few days.

faqs.q: Sunset cruise at the US?
faqs.a: Most listings offer a 2h sunset option. Common on pontoons and sport yachts.

faqs.q: How much does it cost to rent a boat at Lake Powell?
faqs.a: Half-day rates start around $350 for a 22 ft pontoon and run to $2,000 for a 40 ft sport yacht. Full-day rates roughly 50–80% higher. Captain (if needed) is $180–$280/day on top. Fuel pass-through. Most Lake Powell renters spend $600–$1,200 for a half-day group outing.

faqs.q: Do I need a licence or boating card?
faqs.a: Most US states require a Boating Safety Course Certificate (boating card / NASBLA-approved) for self-drive operators born after a state-specific cutoff date. If you don't have one or don't want to drive, book with captain included.

faqs.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faqs.a: Almost always yes — most owners explicitly allow BYO. Some larger party platforms include a basic cooler with ice; confirm at booking. Glass restrictions apply on most lake boats.

faqs.q: What if the weather is bad?
faqs.a: Operator decision based on forecast wind and waves. If the operator cancels for weather, you get a full refund or a free reschedule. Renter cancellation inside the cancellation window follows the standard policy on the listing.

faqs.q: How many people can a pontoon hold?
faqs.a: 22–24 ft pontoons typically take 8–10 guests; 26–28 ft tritoons take 12–14; large party platforms 16+. Coast Guard capacity is on the placard at the helm. Don't exceed it — both safety and insurance.

faqs.q: How early should I book?
faqs.a: Peak summer weekends (especially holiday weeks): 4–8 weeks ahead. Mid-week peak: 1–2 weeks. Shoulder season: a few days. Top boats sell out earliest.

faqs.q: Are there sunset cruise options at Lake Powell?
faqs.a: Yes — most listings offer a 2-hour sunset cruise option at a flat rate. Common on pontoons and sport yachts. Add catering or beverage package as an extra.

faqs.q: Combien coûte la location de bateau à France ?
faqs.a: De €140 pour une demi-journée sans permis à €3,200+ pour une journée sur un yacht 14 m. La majorité des locations groupes se situent entre €550 et €1,400 la journée complète.

faqs.q: Faut-il un permis bateau ?
faqs.a: Non pour les bateaux ≤ 6 CV (moteur, en eaux intérieures et côtières <2 milles). Oui pour tout bateau supérieur : permis côtier minimum. Sans permis = skipper inclus dans le tarif.

faqs.q: Quelle est la durée idéale de location ?
faqs.a: Demi-journée (4h) pour découvrir, journée (8h) pour un vrai itinéraire avec pause baignade et déjeuner. 2h coucher de soleil pour les couples et apéros.

faqs.q: Puis-je apporter ma nourriture et boissons ?
faqs.a: Oui presque toujours. Quelques opérateurs proposent un service traiteur en option. Confirmer à la réservation.

faqs.q: Que se passe-t-il en cas de mauvais temps ?
faqs.a: Décision du skipper ou de l'opérateur selon prévisions. Annulation côté loueur = remboursement intégral ou report sans frais.

faqs.q: Combien de personnes peut accueillir un bateau à France ?
faqs.a: Sans permis : 4–6 personnes. Semi-rigide 7m : 8–10. Bateau 9m : 10–12. Yacht 12m+ : 10–14. Capacité légale affichée à bord.

faqs.q: Comment annuler ma réservation ?
faqs.a: Politique standard : remboursement intégral 14 jours avant départ, 50% entre 7 et 14 jours, pas de remboursement à moins de 7 jours. Politique précise sur chaque annonce.

title: Norfolk Broads Boat Hire — Day & Week Cruisers | BoatHire24
metaDescription: Norfolk Broads Boat Hire through BoatHire24 covers Norfolk Broads: day cruisers, week boats with cabins, sailing cruisers. Self-drive day cruisers, holiday
h1: Norfolk Broads Boat Hire — Day & Week Cruisers
intro: Norfolk Broads Boat Hire through BoatHire24 covers Norfolk Broads: day cruisers, week boats with cabins, sailing cruisers. Self-drive day cruisers, holiday boats with cabins, sailing yachts — book directly from owners and operators with transparent pricing and instant confirmation.
bodyHtml:
What's on the water at Norfolk Broads

Setting: Norfolk Broads. Typical fleet: day cruisers, week boats with cabins, sailing cruisers. Highlights: How Hill, Horning, Salhouse Broad, Hickling.

  • Day boats (4h–8h) and week-long holiday cruisers
  • Self-drive — no licence required on Norfolk Broads; full handover briefing at hire base
  • Electric and diesel options
  • Weekly cruisers with 2–6 berth cabins, kitchen, WC, shower

Indicative rates (2026)

BoatDay hireWeek hire
4-person day boat (electric)£90–£160n/a
6-person day cruiser£120–£220n/a
4-berth week cruisern/a£1,100–£1,800
6-berth week cruisern/a£1,500–£2,800
8-berth premium cruisern/a£2,400–£4,200

Fuel pass-through (week cruisers). Damage deposit £150–£500.

What's included

  • Full safety briefing and boat handover at the hire base
  • Buoyancy aids, navigation chart, mooring kit
  • Bedding and basic galley equipment (week cruisers)
  • Insurance for the hire period

Best time of year

Peak: late May to early September. Week-long cruisers book up early for school holidays (last week of July to last week of August) — reserve 3–6 months ahead. May, June and September deliver the best weather-to-crowd balance and 20–30% cheaper rates.

Own a boat? List it on BoatHire24

Boat owners on Norfolk Broads can list directly on BoatHire24 — free listing, flat 15% commission, calendar control. See become-a-host or our Borrow A Boat alternative comparison.

faqs.q: Do I need a licence to hire a boat on Norfolk Broads?
faqs.a: No. UK inland waters do not require a recreational boat licence for hirers. The hire base runs a full handover briefing covering controls, mooring, locks (where applicable), and safe operation. You're typically on the water within 30 minutes of arriving.

faqs.q: How much for a week on Norfolk Broads?
faqs.a: Mid-range 4-berth cruiser: £1,100–£1,800 in shoulder season, £1,800–£2,800 in summer school holidays. Premium 6–8 berth: £2,400–£4,200 peak. Includes boat, bedding, basic galley kit. Fuel pass-through.

faqs.q: Can we bring a dog?
faqs.a: Many boats allow dogs (£20–£50 supplement). Filter by 'pet-friendly' on the listing. Some restrictions on size and number — confirm at booking.

faqs.q: Do you provide bedding and towels?
faqs.a: Bedding (duvets, sheets, pillows) included on week cruisers. Towels usually not — bring your own or hire at the base (£3–£5 each).

faqs.q: What about mooring overnight?
faqs.a: Most hire boats can moor at marked moorings on the network (free or low fee). Marina overnight £15–£35 including electric hook-up. Plan a mix to balance scenery and convenience.

faqs.q: When should we book?
faqs.a: Summer school holidays (mid-July to late August): 3–6 months ahead. Shoulder season: 4–8 weeks. Off-peak (October–April): days to a couple of weeks ahead.

faqs.q: Are there short-day options?
faqs.a: Yes — day boats (4–8h hire) on most of the network. Smaller electric and outboard boats for 4–6 people, no overnight option but a great way to test the water before committing to a week.

faqs.q: Do I need a boat licence on Sydney?
faqs.a: Most BBQ pontoons and party boats in NSW and QLD can be driven without a recreational skipper's ticket up to a certain HP limit, after a briefing at the hire base. Higher-power yachts require the relevant state RST or equivalent. Skippered hire bypasses the requirement entirely.

faqs.q: Can we BYO drinks and food?

faqs.q: How many people fit on a BBQ pontoon?

faqs.q: Is sunset cruising popular around Sydney?

faqs.q: What about jet ski and tube tow?
faqs.a: Many BBQ pontoons can tow donuts/tubes (extra hire) and some operators allow jet ski follow-on hire. Confirm operator-by-operator.

faqs.q: Cancellation policy?
faqs.a: Standard: full refund 14+ days out, 50% 7–14 days, no refund inside 7 days. Operator-cancelled (weather): full refund or free reschedule.

faqs.q: Weather backups?
faqs.a: Strong wind warnings (35 kn+) typically cancel pontoon hire. Operator decides on the day. You get refund or reschedule at no cost.

bodyHtml:
La flotta a Tropea

Ambiente: costiera tirrenica. Tipologie disponibili: gozzi, barche a motore, semi-rigidi. Attrazioni: calette, grotte, snorkeling.

  • Mezza giornata (4h), giornata intera (8h), tramonto (2h)
  • Con o senza skipper
  • Patente nautica richiesta per natanti oltre 40 CV (sotto: non serve)

Tariffe indicative 2026

TipoMezza giornataGiornata
Gozzo 6m senza patente€140–€220€220–€340
Semi-rigido 7–8m€280–€450€450–€720
Barca a motore 9–11m€600–€950€950–€1,500
Yacht 12–14m€1,200–€2,000€2,000–€3,400
Barca a vela 38–45 ft€450–€750€750–€1,300

Skipper €180–€280/giorno. Carburante a parte. Cauzione €500–€2,500 secondo il natante.

Cosa è incluso

  • Briefing di sicurezza al molo
  • Salvagenti, kit di sicurezza
  • Assicurazione durante il noleggio (listing operatori)
  • Skipper se prenotato

Migliore periodo

Alta stagione da metà giugno a metà settembre. Picco di domanda 1–25 agosto: prenota con 4–6 settimane di anticipo. Maggio, giugno e fine settembre offrono mare più calmo, meno affollamento, tariffe ridotte del 20–30%.

faqs.q: Quanto costa noleggiare una barca a Tropea?
faqs.a: Da €140 per mezza giornata su gozzo senza patente fino a €3,400+ per giornata intera su yacht 14m. Maggior parte dei gruppi spendono €450–€1,500 per giornata intera.

faqs.q: Serve la patente nautica?
faqs.a: Non per natanti fino a 40 CV in acque interne e a non più di 6 miglia dalla costa. Serve patente nautica entro 12 miglia (e per qualsiasi natante >40 CV). Senza patente = skipper incluso.

faqs.q: Posso portare cibo e bevande?
faqs.a: Sì, quasi sempre. Alcuni operatori offrono catering opzionale. Conferma alla prenotazione.

faqs.q: Cosa succede in caso di maltempo?
faqs.a: Decisione dello skipper o dell'operatore in base alle previsioni meteo. Annullamento operatore = rimborso integrale o rinvio gratuito.

faqs.q: Quante persone possono salire a bordo?
faqs.a: Gozzo 6m: 4–6 persone. Semi-rigido 7m: 8–10. Barca 9m: 10–12. Yacht 12m+: 10–14. Capacità legale indicata a bordo.

faqs.q: Quanto in anticipo prenotare?
faqs.a: Agosto: 6–8 settimane prima per i migliori bateau. Luglio: 3–4 settimane. Bassa stagione: pochi giorni.

faqs.q: Politica di cancellazione?
faqs.a: Standard: rimborso integrale 14+ giorni prima, 50% tra 7–14 giorni, nessun rimborso entro 7 giorni. Maltempo = rimborso integrale.
